content: Elderly health-monitoring value is real (49% of seniors in a 2025 US News survey feel safer with assistive/alert tech) but genuine fall detection and continuous heart-rate monitoring rely on body-worn wearables/accelerometer placement, not phone-in-pocket sensing. Phone-only accelerometer fall/crash detection exists (see Life360/OtoZen crash detection at driving speeds) but is a lower-fidelity, distinct use case from wearable-grade elder fall detection.
